node_types.description: A person receiving psychotherapy, with attributes relevant to Islamic-cultural alignment.

properties.name: religious_identity
properties.description: Client's self-identified religious affiliation or orientation

properties.name: spiritual_orientation
properties.description: Client's level and style of spirituality or religiosity

properties.name: cultural_background
properties.description: Client's cultural, ethnic, and community context

properties.name: presenting_problem
properties.description: Primary psychological or relational issues presented

properties.name: family_structure
properties.description: Family composition and roles relevant to therapy

properties.name: community_context
properties.description: Community, social, and environmental influences

properties.name: readiness_for_spiritual_integration
properties.description: Client's openness to integrating Islamic or spiritual concepts in therapy

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 1: An Islamic Theoretical Orientation to Psychotherapy
Chapter 2: Utilization of Islamic Principles in Marital Counseling
Chapter 3: The HEART Method
Chapter 4: Conducting Spiritually Integrated Family Therapy
Chapter 5: Integrating Islamic Spirituality into Psychodynamic Therapy
Chapter 6: Family Therapy and the Use of Quranic Stories
Chapter 7: Outlining a Case Illustration of Traditional Islamically Integrated Psychotherapy
Chapter 8: Marrying Islamic Principles with Western Psychotherapy for Children and Adolescents
Chapter 9: Integrating Duaa Arafa and Other Shiite Teachings into Psychotherapy

node_types.description: A mental health professional providing therapy, with attributes relevant to Islamic-cultural competence.

properties.name: religious_identity
properties.description: Therapist's self-identified religious affiliation or orientation

properties.description: Therapist's personal spiritual practices and development

properties.name: cultural_competence
properties.description: Therapist's training and experience in Islamic-cultural alignment

properties.name: language_skills
properties.description: Languages spoken by the therapist

properties.name: theoretical_orientation
properties.description: Therapist's primary therapeutic models and approaches

node_types.description: A concept, value, or practice from Islamic tradition relevant to psychotherapy.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Name of the Islamic concept (e.g., tawakkul, nafs, qalb)

properties.name: category
properties.description: Type of concept (e.g., spiritual state, practice, value, explanatory model)

properties.name: description
properties.description: Explanation of the concept

properties.name: sectarian_context
properties.description: Sunni, Shiite, or universal Islamic context

properties.name: scriptural_sources
properties.description: Quranic verses, hadith, or other sources supporting the concept

node_types.description: A specific intervention, technique, or practice used in therapy, including Islamically integrated methods.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Name of the intervention (e.g., dhikr, muraqaba, cognitive restructuring)

properties.name: modality
properties.description: Type of intervention (e.g., cognitive, behavioral, spiritual, systemic)

properties.name: description
properties.description: Explanation of the intervention

properties.name: islamic_basis
properties.description: Islamic concepts or sources underlying the intervention

properties.name: applicability
properties.description: Contexts or client types for which the intervention is suitable
